nativeRange.habitat: Anubias Minima grows naturally along shaded forest streams and riverbanks across West and Central Africa, attaching to rocks and submerged wood in conditions that leave the plant partially or fully submerged.

decorPreferences: porous rock (lava, dragon stone) for attachment

summary: A compact West African Anubias distinguished by its slender, narrow-pointed dark-green leaves. Generally an easy, slow-growing epiphyte well suited to midground placement attached to driftwood or rock.
careNotes: Anubias minima is a smaller West African Anubias notable for its distinctive long, narrow, pointed leaves — visibly more slender than the rounded leaves of A. nana 'Petite', and a different plant entirely despite the shared 'dwarf' framing some retailers use. Taxonomically it is now generally treated as a synonym of Anubias barteri var. glabra, though A. minima remains the dominant trade name. Plants in established tanks typically reach about 15-25 cm in overall height; leaf blades can run 6-21 cm long. As with all Anubias, it is an epiphyte: the rhizome should be attached to driftwood or porous rock (super glue gel, fishing line, or wedging) and kept above the substrate — burying the rhizome has been reported to cause the plant to melt and rot. Best suited to midground placement where its narrow leaves can be appreciated against the hardscape. Tolerates a wide light range but is slow-growing and tends to accumulate algae under high light; low-to-medium light is the safer default. CO2 is optional but generally improves growth and reduces algae pressure. Generally advised to keep around 22-28°C with stable, well-cycled water — sudden parameter swings have been reported to trigger leaf melt. Liquid fertilization (especially iron and trace elements) is helpful since the plant draws nutrients from the water column rather than the substrate. Native to West and Central African forest streams (Guinea through the Democratic Republic of the Congo).

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source: confirmed temperature tolerance 68-82°F (20-28°C), low-to-moderate lighting, beginner/easy care, foreground-to-midground placement, attachment to rocks or driftwood, and the rhizome-must-not-be-buried warning.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name (Anubias minima), West African origin, adult size 6-8 inches (15-20 cm), pH 6.0-7.5, low lighting, slow-to-moderate growth, no-CO2-required, epiphyte habit with rhizome attachment, propagation by rhizome division, and the distinguishing 'long and slender leaves in contrast to Anubias nana' note.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback: family Araceae, order Alismatales, treats A. minima as a synonym of Anubias barteri var. glabra. Confirmed native countries (Guinea, Liberia, Ivory Coast, Cameroon, Bioko/Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, DRC), leaf-blade dimensions (6-21 cm long, 1.5-9 cm wide), and temperature preference 22-28°C.
